free_text: .No details given. Effects from foil thickness were
less than 0.3%, according to the authors.
.Boron pile. Fission neutrons slowed down in a
220 cm side cubic core of a graphite stack,
surrounded by a 35 cm thick graphite reflector to
keep them inside. The whole is surrounded by a
neutron shield consisting of 0.4 mm cadmium inside
2 BF3-counters in each hole of the lattice
of 11*11 holes in the graphite stack of the boron
pile. 5 cm diam detectors, enriched boron,
fission chamber in the centre of the boron
For measuring the monitor reaction. It
full description of the boron pile.

.A number of pitfalls is discussed in the article,
e.g. A 50 Hz modulation of the Van de Graaff beam
by the magnetic field of the belt motors, anisotropy
of the efficiency, (n,f)-reactions induced by the
discussed in reference, viz. count-rate effect,
overlap correction, effect of fission chamber,
ionization chamber and beam tube on the boron pile
efficiency, neutrons captured after the gate for
counting the fission neutrons is closed, the
variation of efficiency with the position in the
central channel, effects of impurities in the
fission foils, correction for delayed neutrons.
